revision.text: '''境王子'''（さかいおうじ）は[[九十九王子]]の7番目。名前の由来は[[摂津]]、[[河内]]、[[和泉]]の境にあることからおこった、堺の地名からである。現存していない。跡地は[[堺市]]北田出井町３－４６の王子ヶ飢公園で、そこには[[熊野三山]]の首・[[熊野本宮大社]]の鎮座する[[和歌山県]][[本宮町]]から贈られた「境王子跡」の石碑がたっている。公園のすぐそこは大阪刑務所である。現在は堺市戎之町東2-1-38の[[菅原道真]]を祀る菅原神社に合祀されている。

'''境王子'''（さかいおうじ）は[[九十九王子]]の7番目。名前の由来は[[摂津]]、[[河内]]、[[和泉]]の境にあることからおこった、堺の地名からである。現存していない。跡地は[[堺市]]北田出井町３－４６の王子ヶ飢公園で、そこには[[熊野三山]]の首・[[熊野本宮大社]]の鎮座する[[和歌山県]][[本宮町]]から贈られた「境王子跡」の石碑がたっている。公園のすぐそこは大阪刑務所である。現在は堺市戎之町東2-1-38の[[菅原道真]]を祀る菅原神社に合祀されている。
*王子ヶ飢公園、菅原神社の最寄駅 [[南海電鉄]][[高野線]][[堺東駅]]

'''境王子'''（'''さかいおうじ'''）は、[[熊野街道]]沿いに設けられた[[九十九王子]]の一つ。[[窪津王子]]から数えて7番目にあたる。
== 位置 == 境王子は現在の[[堺市]]にあったが、堺市熊野町の熊野神社に移り（時期不詳）、その熊野神社も[[1908年]]（明治41年）6月に菅原神社（堺市戎之町東2-1-38）に合祀されたため、正確な位置は不明である。
しかし、文献などから、大坂から南下してきた熊野街道は堺の町の東側を通り、[[方違神社]]付近で[[長尾街道]]（大津道）と交差し、[[反正天皇]]陵の西側を通っていたことはわかっており、境王子は[[方違神社]]（[[堺市]]北三国ヶ丘町）の北方付近にあったと考えられている。この地域は三国山などと呼ばれ、[[摂津国|摂津]]、[[河内国|河内]]、[[和泉国|和泉]]の境があった場所で、境王子の名はこれに由来する。 <!--参考：菅原神社御由緒略記、角川地名辞典「大阪府」ほか-->
これらのことから、現在では王子ヶ飢公園（[[堺市]]北田出井町3-46）に「境王子跡」の石碑が建っている。これは[[熊野本宮大社]]の鎮座する[[和歌山県]][[本宮町 (和歌山県)|本宮町]]（現[[田辺市]]）から贈られたものである。
== 交通 ==
*王子ヶ飢公園：[[南海電気鉄道|南海電鉄]][[南海高野線|高野線]][[堺東駅]]から北東へ徒歩 *菅原神社：[[阪堺電気軌道|阪堺電軌]][[阪堺線]][[花田口駅]]駅から東南へ徒歩
{{shinto-stub}} [[Category:大阪府の神社|さかいおうし]] [[Category:堺市|さかいおうし]] [[Category:九十九王子|さかいおうし]]

'''境王子'''（'''さかいおうじ'''）は、[[熊野街道]]沿いに設けられた[[九十九王子]]の一つ。[[窪津王子]]から数えて7番目にあたる。
== 位置 == 境王子は現在の[[堺市]]三国ヶ丘付近にあったと推定されている。この地域は、[[摂津国|摂津]]、[[河内国|河内]]、[[和泉国|和泉]]の境があった場所で、境王子の名はこれに由来する。
王子社は、後世に堺市熊野町の熊野神社（現在の堺市熊野町東1丁の大道筋沿い）に移り（時期不詳）、その熊野神社も[[1908年]]（明治41年）6月に菅原神社（堺市戎之町東2-1-38）に合祀されたため、正確な位置は不明である。
しかし、文献などから、大坂から南下してきた熊野街道は堺の町の東側を通り、[[方違神社]]付近で[[長尾街道]]（大津道）と交差し、[[反正天皇]]陵の西側を通っていたことはわかっており、境王子は[[方違神社]]（[[堺市]]北三国ヶ丘町）の北方付近にあったと考えられている。実際、文久三年の堺絵図には「小栗道」「王子ヶ飢」の記載があり、境王子がかつてあったことがうかがえる。 <!--参考：菅原神社御由緒略記、角川地名辞典「大阪府」ほか-->
これらのことから、現在は王子ヶ飢公園（[[堺市]]北田出井町3-46）に「境王子跡」の石碑が建っている。これは[[熊野本宮大社]]の鎮座する[[和歌山県]][[本宮町 (和歌山県)|本宮町]]（現[[田辺市]]）から贈られたものである。
== 交通 == *王子ヶ飢公園：[[南海電気鉄道|南海電鉄]][[南海高野線|高野線]][[堺東駅]]から北東へ徒歩 *菅原神社：[[阪堺電気軌道|阪堺電軌]][[阪堺線]][[花田口駅]]駅から東南へ徒歩
{{shinto-stub}} [[Category:大阪府の神社|さかいおうし]] [[Category:堺市|さかいおうし]] [[Category:九十九王子|さかいおうし]]

これらのことから、現在は王子ヶ飢公園（[[堺市]]北田出井町3-46）に「境王子跡」の石碑が建っている。これは[[熊野本宮大社]]の鎮座する[[和歌山県]][[本宮町 (和歌山県)|本宮町]]（現[[田辺市]]）から贈られたものである。

'''境王子'''（'''さかいおうじ'''）は、[[熊野街道]]沿いに設けられた[[九十九王子]]の一つ。[[窪津王子]]から数えて7番目にあたる。
== 位置 == 境王子は現在の[[堺市]]堺区三国ヶ丘町付近にあったと推定されている。この地域は、[[摂津国|摂津]]、[[河内国|河内]]、[[和泉国|和泉]]の境があった場所で、境王子の名はこれに由来する。
王子社は、後世に堺市熊野町の熊野神社（現在の堺区熊野町東1丁の大道筋沿い）に移り（時期不詳）、その熊野神社も[[1908年]]（明治41年）6月に菅原神社（堺区戎之町東2-1-38）に合祀されたため、正確な位置は不明である。
しかし、文献などから、大坂から南下してきた熊野街道は堺の町の東側を通り、[[方違神社]]付近で[[長尾街道]]（大津道）と交差し、[[反正天皇]]陵の西側を通っていたことはわかっており、境王子は[[方違神社]]（堺区北三国ヶ丘町）の北方付近にあったと考えられている。実際、文久三年の堺絵図には「小栗道」「王子ヶ飢」の記載があり、境王子がかつてあったことがうかがえる。 <!--参考：菅原神社御由緒略記、角川地名辞典「大阪府」ほか-->
これらのことから、現在は王子ヶ飢公園（堺区北田出井町3-46）に「境王子跡」の石碑が建っている。これは[[熊野本宮大社]]の鎮座する[[和歌山県]][[本宮町 (和歌山県)|本宮町]]（現[[田辺市]]）から贈られたものである。

revision.comment: 『日本史大事典』（平凡社）第3巻「堺」（執筆:菅原憲二）を参考に加筆
revision.text:
'''境王子'''（さかいおうじ）は、[[熊野街道]]沿いに設けられた[[九十九王子]]の一つ。[[窪津王子]]から数えて7番目にあたる。
== 位置 == 境王子は現在の[[堺市]][[堺区]]三国ヶ丘町付近にあったと推定されている。この地域は、[[摂津国|摂津]]、[[河内国|河内]]、[[和泉国|和泉]]の境があった場所で、境王子の名はこれに由来する。また、「堺」（「境」の別表記）という地名も境王子が置かれた平安時代後期から歴史文献に登場するようになり（[[1081年]]（永保元年）の『[[藤原為房卿記]]』には境王子が「和泉堺之小堂」という名称で登場し、これが「堺」という地名の初出である）、境王子がその由来となったと考えられている。
王子社は、後世に堺市熊野町の熊野神社（現在の堺区熊野町東1丁の大道筋沿い）に移り（時期不詳）、その熊野神社も[[1908年]]（明治41年）6月に菅原神社（堺区戎之町東2-1-38）に合祀されたため、正確な位置は不明である。
しかし、文献などから、大坂から南下してきた熊野街道は堺の町の東側を通り、[[方違神社]]付近で[[長尾街道]]（大津道）と交差し、[[反正天皇]]陵の西側を通っていたことはわかっており、境王子は[[方違神社]]（堺区北三国ヶ丘町）の北方付近にあったと考えられている。実際、文久三年の堺絵図には「小栗道」「王子ヶ飢」の記載があり、境王子がかつてあったことがうかがえる。 <!--参考：菅原神社御由緒略記、角川地名辞典「大阪府」ほか-->
これらのことから、現在は王子ヶ飢公園（堺区北田出井町3-46）に「境王子跡」の石碑が建っている。これは[[熊野本宮大社]]の鎮座する[[和歌山県]][[本宮町 (和歌山県)|本宮町]]（現[[田辺市]]）から贈られたものである。
== 交通 == *王子ヶ飢公園：[[南海電気鉄道|南海電鉄]][[南海高野線|高野線]][[堺東駅]]から北東へ徒歩 *菅原神社：[[阪堺電気軌道|阪堺電軌]][[阪堺電気軌道阪堺線|阪堺線]][[花田口駅]]駅から東南へ徒歩
== 関連項目 == * [[熊野信仰]] * [[熊野古道]] - [[紀伊路]] * [[九十九王子]] : [[津守王子]] - '''境王子''' - [[大鳥居王子]]
{{shinto-stub}} [[Category:堺市の神社|さかいおうし]] [[Category:堺区|さかいおうし]] [[Category:九十九王子|さかいおうし]]

'''境王子'''（さかいおうじ）は、[[熊野街道]]沿いに設けられた[[九十九王子]]の一つ。[[窪津王子]]から数えて7番目にあたる。
== 位置 == 境王子は現在の[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[堺区]]北田出井町付近にあったと推定されている。この地域は、[[摂津国]]、[[河内国]]、[[和泉国]]の境があった場所で、境王子の名はこれに由来する。また、「堺」（「境」の別表記）という地名も境王子が置かれた平安時代後期から歴史文献に登場するようになり（[[1081年]]（[[永保]]元年）の『[[藤原為房卿記]]』には境王子が「和泉堺之小堂」という名称で登場し、これが「堺」という地名の初出である）、境王子がその由来となったと考えられている。
王子社は、後世に堺湯屋町の熊野神社（現在の堺区熊野町東1丁の[[大道筋]]沿い）に移り（時期不詳）、その熊野神社も[[1908年]]（[[明治]]41年）6月に[[菅原神社 (堺市)|菅原神社]]（堺区戎之町東2丁1-38）に合祀されたため、正確な位置は不明である。
しかし、文献などから、大坂から南下してきた熊野街道は堺の町の東側を通り、[[方違神社]]（堺区北三国ヶ丘町2丁2-1）付近で[[長尾街道]]（大津道）と交差し、[[反正天皇]]陵の西側を通っていたことはわかっており、境王子は方違神社の北方付近にあったと考えられている。実際、『[[文久]]三年改正堺大絵図』には「小栗道」「王子ヶ飢」の記載があり、境王子がかつてあったことがうかがえる。 <!--参考：菅原神社御由緒略記、角川地名辞典「大阪府」ほか-->
これらのことから、現在は王子ヶ飢公園（堺区北田出井町3丁4-6）に「境王子跡」の石碑が建っている。これは[[熊野本宮大社]]の鎮座する[[和歌山県]][[本宮町 (和歌山県)|本宮町]]（現[[田辺市]]）から贈られたものである。

== 位置 == 境王子は現在の[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[堺区]]北田出井町付近にあったと推定されている。この地域は、[[摂津国]]、[[河内国]]、[[和泉国]]の境があった場所で、境王子の名はこれに由来する。また、「堺」（「境」の別表記）という地名も境王子が置かれた平安時代後期から歴史文献に登場するようになり（[[1081年]]（[[永保]]元年）の『[[藤原為房卿記]]』には境王子が「和泉堺之小堂」という名称で登場し、これが「堺」という地名の初出である）、境王子がその由来となったと考えられている。
王子社は、後世に堺湯屋町の熊野神社（現在の堺区熊野町東1丁の[[大道筋]]沿い）に移り（時期不詳）、その熊野神社も[[1908年]]（[[明治]]41年）6月に[[菅原神社 (堺市)|菅原神社]]（堺区戎之町東2丁1-38）に合祀されたため、正確な位置は不明である。
しかし、文献などから、大坂から南下してきた熊野街道は堺の町の東側を通り、[[方違神社]]（堺区北三国ヶ丘町2丁2-1）付近で[[長尾街道]]（大津道）と交差し、[[反正天皇]]陵の西側を通っていたことはわかっており、境王子は方違神社の北方付近にあったと考えられている。実際、『[[文久]]三年改正堺大絵図』には「小栗道」「王子ヶ飢」の記載があり、境王子がかつてあったことがうかがえる。 <!--参考：菅原神社御由緒略記、角川地名辞典「大阪府」ほか-->
これらのことから、現在は王子ヶ飢公園（堺区北田出井町3丁4-6）に「境王子跡」の石碑が建っている。これは[[熊野本宮大社]]の鎮座する[[和歌山県]][[本宮町 (和歌山県)|本宮町]]（現[[田辺市]]）から贈られたものである。
== 交通 == *王子ヶ飢公園 - [[南海電気鉄道|南海電鉄]][[南海高野線|高野線]][[堺東駅]]から北東へ徒歩 *[[菅原神社 (堺市)|菅原神社]] - [[阪堺電気軌道|阪堺電軌]][[阪堺電気軌道阪堺線|阪堺線]][[花田口停留場]]駅から東南へ徒歩

しかし、文献などから、大坂から南下してきた熊野街道は堺の町の東側を通り、[[方違神社]]（堺区北三国ヶ丘町2丁2-1）付近で[[長尾街道]]（大津道）と交差し、[[反正天皇]]陵の西側を通っていたことはわかっており、境王子は方違神社の北方付近にあったと考えられている。実際、『[[文久]]三年改正堺大絵図』には「小栗道」「王子ヶ飢」の記載があり、境王子がかつてあったことがうかがえる。 <!--参考：菅原神社御由緒略記、角川地名辞典「大阪府」ほか-->
これらのことから、現在は王子ヶ飢公園（堺区北田出井町3丁4-6）に「境王子跡」の石碑が建っている。これは[[熊野本宮大社]]の鎮座する[[和歌山県]][[本宮町 (和歌山県)|本宮町]]（現[[田辺市]]）から贈られたものである。
{{Gallery |ファイル:境王子跡.jpg|境王子跡 |ファイル:境王子跡(説明).jpg|境王子跡の説明 }}
== 交通 == *王子ヶ飢公園 - [[南海電気鉄道|南海電鉄]][[南海高野線|高野線]][[堺東駅]]から北東へ約850m。 **堺東駅より難波方面へ向かって線路沿いに北上し３つ目の踏切を東入りすぐ。 *[[菅原神社 (堺市)|菅原神社]] - [[阪堺電気軌道|阪堺電軌]][[阪堺電気軌道阪堺線|阪堺線]][[花田口停留場]]駅から東南へ約300m。

revision.comment: Adding an intro to all of them. It has a magic word instead of a substitution because all of them are gonna be moved in the future to proper pagenames. See comment for more info.
revision.text:
''{{PAGENAME}}'' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group ''{{PAGENAME}}'' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group '''{{PAGENAME}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | Image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | Location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (さかいおうじ) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage routes.
== Location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * **Ōji-ga-ue Park:** About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. * From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * **{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:** About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} - '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}

'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | Image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | Location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (さかいおうじ) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage routes.
== Location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * **Ōji-ga-ue Park:** About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. * From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * **{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:** About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} - '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}

revision.text:
'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | Image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | Location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (さかいおうじ) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage routes.
== Location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * **Ōji-ga-ue Park:** About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. * From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * **{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:** About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} - '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}
{{Shinto Sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}}

'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | Image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | Location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (さかいおうじ) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage routes.
== Location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * **Ōji-ga-ue Park:** About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. * From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * **{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:** About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} - '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}

'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | Image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | Location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (さかいおうじ) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage routes.
== Location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * **Ōji-ga-ue Park:** About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. * From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * **{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:** About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} - '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}

'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | Image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | Location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (さかいおうじ) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage routes.
== Location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * **Ōji-ga-ue Park:** About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. * From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * **{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:** About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} - '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}

'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | Image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | Location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (さかいおうじ) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage routes.
== Location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * **Ōji-ga-ue Park:** About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. * From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * **{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:** About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} - '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}

'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | Image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | Location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (さかいおうじ) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage routes.
== Location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * **Ōji-ga-ue Park:** About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. * From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * **{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:** About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} - '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}

Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.

== Access == * **Ōji-ga-ue Park:** About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. * From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * **{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:** About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.

revision.text:
'''Sakai Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | Image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | Location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (さかいおうじ) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage routes.
== Location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * **Ōji-ga-ue Park:** About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. * From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * **{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:** About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} - '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}

revision.text:
'''Sakai Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | Image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | Location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (さかいおうじ) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage routes.
== Location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * **Ōji-ga-ue Park:** About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. * From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * **{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:** About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} - '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}
{{Shinto Sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
'''Sakai Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | Image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | Location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (さかいおうじ) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage routes.
== Location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * **Ōji-ga-ue Park:** About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. * From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * **{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:** About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} - '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}

revision.text:
'''Sakai Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | Image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | Location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (さかいおうじ) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage routes.
== Location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * **Ōji-ga-ue Park:** About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. * From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * **{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:** About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} - '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}

'''Sakai Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | Image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | Location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (さかいおうじ) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage routes.
== Location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * **Ōji-ga-ue Park:** About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. * From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * **{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:** About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} - '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}

== Access == * **Ōji-ga-ue Park:** About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. * From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * **{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:** About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.

'''Sakai Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | Image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | Location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (さかいおうじ) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage routes.
== Location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * **Ōji-ga-ue Park:** About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. * From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * **{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:** About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} - '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}

'''Sakai Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | Image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | Location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (さかいおうじ) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage routes.
== Location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * **Ōji-ga-ue Park:** About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. * From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * **{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:** About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} - '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}

'''Sakai Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | Image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | Location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (さかいおうじ) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage routes.
== Location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * **Ōji-ga-ue Park:** About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. * From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * **{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:** About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} - '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}

'''Sakai Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | Image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | Location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (さかいおうじ) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage routes.
== Location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * **Ōji-ga-ue Park:** About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. * From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * **{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:** About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} - '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}

'''Sakai Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | Image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | Location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (さかいおうじ) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage routes.
== Location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * **Ōji-ga-ue Park:** About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. * From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * **{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:** About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} - '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}

'''Sakai Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | Image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | Location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (さかいおうじ) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage routes.
== Location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
