'''Sakai Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (さかいおうじ) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. * From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} - '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}

'''Sakai Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (さかいおうじ) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. * From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} - '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}

'''Sakai Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (さかいおうじ) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. * From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} - '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}

[[Category:Kujūku Ōji template forks]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]]<!--Sakai Ōji-->[[Category:last 150 pages as of feb qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakai Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Sakai Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakai Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakai Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:goodbye $1]][[sn:Sakai Ōji]][[Category:goodbye $1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakai Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakai Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakai Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakai Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:akai Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]][[sn:Sakai Ōji]][[Category:qqq]] ]] [[ja:境王子]][[zh:境王子]][[zh:九十九王子 (有田市)]] {{Kujūku Ōji}} [[sn:akai Ōji]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Sakai Ōji]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]]

'''Sakai Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (さかいおうじ) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. * From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} - '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}

'''Sakai Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (さかいおうじ) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. * From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} - '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}

The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. * From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.

'''Sakai Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (さかいおうじ) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.

== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. * From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.

revision.text:
'''Sakai Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (さかいおうじ) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. * From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} - '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}

'''Sakai Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (さかいおうじ) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. * From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} - '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}

'''Sakai Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (さかいおうじ) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. * From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} - '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}

[[zh:九十九王子 (有田市)]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakai Ōji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]]<!--Sakai Ōji-->[[en:Sakai Ōji]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]]<!--Sakai Ōji-->{{citation needed|bottom}}[[Category:s11]]
[[Category:Kujūku Ōji template forks]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]]<!--Sakai Ōji-->[[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakai Ōji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Sakai Ōji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakai Ōji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakai Ōji]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Sakai Ōji]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakai Ōji]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakai Ōji]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakai Ōji]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakai Ōji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:akai Ōji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakai Ōji]][[Category:11]] ]] [[ja:境王子]][[zh:境王子]][[zh:九十九王子 (有田市)]] {{Kujūku Ōji}} [[sn:akai Ōji]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Sakai Ōji]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakai Ōji]][[Category:11]]

'''Sakai Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (さかいおうじ) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. * From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} - '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}

'''Sakai Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (さかいおうじ) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. * From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} * '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}

The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. ** From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.

'''Sakai Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (さかいおうじ) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. ** From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.

revision.text:
'''Sakai Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (さかいおうじ) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. ** From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} * '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}

revision.text:
'''Sakai Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (さかいおうじ) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. ** From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} * '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}

'''Sakai Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (さかいおうじ) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. ** From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} * '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|九十九王子" to "|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji"
revision.text:
'''Sakai Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (さかいおうじ) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. ** From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} * '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "九十九王子|" to "九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|"
revision.text:
'''Sakai Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (さかいおうじ) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. ** From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} * '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}

The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. ** From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (境王子) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.

== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. ** From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (境王子) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社 (堺市)|lt=Sugawara Shrine}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. ** From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} * '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}
