{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (境王子) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社 (堺市)|lt=Sugawara Shrine}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. ** From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} * '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (境王子) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社 (堺市)|lt=Sugawara Shrine}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. ** From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} * '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (境王子) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社 (堺市)|lt=Sugawara Shrine}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. ** From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} * '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}]] | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' (境王子) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社 (堺市)|lt=Sugawara Shrine}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. ** From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} * '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}

References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|ja|永保}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)|ja|熊野神社}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社 (堺市)|lt=Sugawara Shrine}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|田辺市}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|ja|南海電気鉄道}}. ** From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|ja|阪堺電気軌道阪堺線}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|ja|阪堺電気軌道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子}} * '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}

revision.text:
{{出典の明記| date = 2024年11月}} '''境王子'''（さかいおうじ）は、[[熊野街道]]沿いに設けられた[[九十九王子]]の一つ。[[窪津王子]]から数えて7番目にあたる。
== 位置 == 境王子は現在の[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[堺区]]北田出井町付近にあったと推定されている。この地域は、[[摂津国]]、[[河内国]]、[[和泉国]]の境があった場所で、境王子の名はこれに由来する。また、「堺」（「境」の別表記）という地名も境王子が置かれた平安時代後期から歴史文献に登場するようになり（[[1081年]]（[[永保]]元年）の『藤原[[為房卿記]]』には境王子が「和泉堺之小堂」という名称で登場し、これが「堺」という地名の初出である）、境王子がその由来となったと考えられている。
王子社は、後世に堺湯屋町の熊野神社（現在の堺区熊野町東1丁の[[大道筋]]沿い）に移り（時期不詳）、その熊野神社も[[1908年]]（[[明治]]41年）6月に[[菅原神社 (堺市)|菅原神社]]（堺区戎之町東2丁1-38）に合祀されたため、正確な位置は不明である。
しかし、文献などから、大坂から南下してきた熊野街道は堺の町の東側を通り、[[方違神社]]（堺区北三国ヶ丘町2丁2-1）付近で[[長尾街道]]（大津道）と交差し、[[反正天皇]]陵の西側を通っていたことはわかっており、境王子は方違神社の北方付近にあったと考えられている。実際、『[[文久]]三年改正堺大絵図』には「小栗道」「王子ヶ飢」の記載があり、境王子がかつてあったことがうかがえる。 <!--参考：菅原神社御由緒略記、角川地名辞典「大阪府」ほか-->
これらのことから、現在は王子ヶ飢公園（堺区北田出井町3丁4-6）に「境王子跡」の石碑が建っている。これは[[熊野本宮大社]]の鎮座する[[和歌山県]][[本宮町 (和歌山県)|本宮町]]（現[[田辺市]]）から贈られたものである。
{{Gallery |ファイル:境王子跡.jpg|境王子跡 }}
== 交通 == *王子ヶ飢公園 - [[南海電気鉄道|南海電鉄]][[南海高野線|高野線]][[堺東駅]]から北東へ約850m。 **堺東駅より難波方面へ向かって線路沿いに北上し3つ目の踏切を東入りすぐ。 *[[菅原神社 (堺市)|菅原神社]] - [[阪堺電気軌道|阪堺電軌]][[阪堺電気軌道阪堺線|阪堺線]][[花田口停留場]]駅から東南へ約300m。
== 関連項目 == *[[熊野三山]] *[[熊野古道]] - [[紀伊路]] *[[九十九王子]] *:[[津守王子]] - '''境王子''' - [[大鳥居王子]]
{{神道 横}}
{{shinto-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:さかいおうし}} [[Category:堺市の神社]] [[Category:堺区の歴史]] [[Category:九十九王子]] [[Category:堺区の建築物]]

revision.text:
{{出典の明記| date = 2024年11月}} '''境王子'''（さかいおうじ）は、[[熊野街道]]沿いに設けられた[[九十九王子]]の一つ。[[窪津王子]]から数えて7番目にあたる。
== 位置 == 境王子は現在の[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[堺区]]北田出井町付近にあったと推定されている。この地域は、[[摂津国]]、[[河内国]]、[[和泉国]]の境があった場所で、境王子の名はこれに由来する。また、「堺」（「境」の別表記）という地名も境王子が置かれた平安時代後期から歴史文献に登場するようになり（[[1081年]]（[[永保]]元年）の『藤原[[為房卿記]]』には境王子が「和泉堺之小堂」という名称で登場し、これが「堺」という地名の初出である）、境王子がその由来となったと考えられている。
王子社は、後世に堺湯屋町の熊野神社（現在の堺区熊野町東1丁の[[大道筋]]沿い）に移り（時期不詳）、その熊野神社も[[1908年]]（[[明治]]41年）6月に[[菅原神社 (堺市)|菅原神社]]（堺区戎之町東2丁1-38）に合祀されたため、正確な位置は不明である。
しかし、文献などから、大坂から南下してきた熊野街道は堺の町の東側を通り、[[方違神社]]（堺区北三国ヶ丘町2丁2-1）付近で[[長尾街道]]（大津道）と交差し、[[反正天皇]]陵の西側を通っていたことはわかっており、境王子は方違神社の北方付近にあったと考えられている。実際、『[[文久]]三年改正堺大絵図』には「小栗道」「王子ヶ飢」の記載があり、境王子がかつてあったことがうかがえる。 <!--参考：菅原神社御由緒略記、角川地名辞典「大阪府」ほか-->
これらのことから、現在は王子ヶ飢公園（堺区北田出井町3丁4-6）に「境王子跡」の石碑が建っている。これは[[熊野本宮大社]]の鎮座する[[和歌山県]][[本宮町 (和歌山県)|本宮町]]（現[[田辺市]]）から贈られたものである。
{{Gallery |ファイル:境王子跡.jpg|境王子跡 }}
== 交通 == *王子ヶ飢公園 - [[南海電気鉄道|南海電鉄]][[南海高野線|高野線]][[堺東駅]]から北東へ約850m。 **堺東駅より難波方面へ向かって線路沿いに北上し3つ目の踏切を東入りすぐ。 *[[菅原神社 (堺市)|菅原神社]] - [[阪堺電気軌道|阪堺電軌]][[阪堺電気軌道阪堺線|阪堺線]][[花田口停留場]]駅から東南へ約300m。
== 関連項目 == *[[熊野三山]] *[[熊野古道]] - [[紀伊路]] *[[九十九王子]] *:[[津守王子]] - '''境王子''' - [[大鳥居王子]]
{{神道 横}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:境王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11429062]] Wikidata name: Sakai-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|qq=|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|qq=|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}]] | location = [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|qid=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|qid=Q1042008}} }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|qq=|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}''' (境王子) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, part of the historical [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitadaidei-chō|qq=|1=Kitadaidei-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131912466}} in [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]]. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], and [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]].
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ([[:en:Eihō|Eihō]] 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)]] in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdōri-suji|qq=|1=Daido-suji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439390}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). In 1908 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社 (堺市)|lt=Sugawara Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|qq=|1=Sugawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619211}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōdō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kaidō|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568976}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagao Kaidō|qq=|1=Nagao Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651896}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōtate Shrine|qq=|1=Hochigai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504380}}, and passed west of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Hanzei|Hanzei]]'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-ga-ue Park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|qq=|1=Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520136}}, now part of [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]].
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|qq=}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from [[:en:Sakaihigashi Station|Sakaihigashi Station]] on the [[:en:Koya Line|Nankai Kōya Line]], operated by [[:en:Nankai Electric Railway|Nankai Electric Railway]]. ** From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|qq=|1=Sugawara Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48532247}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanataguchi Station|qq=|1=Hanataguchi Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615569}} on the [[:en:Hankai Line|Hankai Line]], operated by [[:en:Hankai Tramway|Hankai Tramway]].
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori Ōji|qq=|1=Tsumori Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555995}} * '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|qq=|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|qq=|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:境王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11429062]] Wikidata name: Sakai-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|qq=|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|qq=|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}]] | location = [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|WD=Q1042008}} }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|qq=|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}''' (境王子) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, part of the historical [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitadaidei-chō|qq=|1=Kitadaidei-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131912466}} in [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]]. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], and [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]].
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ([[:en:Eihō|Eihō]] 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)]] in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdōri-suji|qq=|1=Daido-suji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439390}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). In 1908 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社 (堺市)|lt=Sugawara Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|qq=|1=Sugawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619211}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōdō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kaidō|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568976}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagao Kaidō|qq=|1=Nagao Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651896}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōtate Shrine|qq=|1=Hochigai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504380}}, and passed west of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Hanzei|Hanzei]]'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-ga-ue Park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|qq=|1=Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520136}}, now part of [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]].
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|qq=}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from [[:en:Sakaihigashi Station|Sakaihigashi Station]] on the [[:en:Koya Line|Nankai Kōya Line]], operated by [[:en:Nankai Electric Railway|Nankai Electric Railway]]. ** From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|qq=|1=Sugawara Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48532247}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanataguchi Station|qq=|1=Hanataguchi Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615569}} on the [[:en:Hankai Line|Hankai Line]], operated by [[:en:Hankai Tramway|Hankai Tramway]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:境王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11429062]] Wikidata name: Sakai-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|qq=|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|qq=|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}]] | location = [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|WD=Q1042008}} }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|qq=|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}''' (境王子) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, part of the historical [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitadaidei-chō|qq=|1=Kitadaidei-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131912466}} in [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]]. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], and [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]].
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ([[:en:Eihō|Eihō]] 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)]] in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdōri-suji|qq=|1=Daido-suji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439390}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). In 1908 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社 (堺市)|lt=Sugawara Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|qq=|1=Sugawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619211}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōdō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kaidō|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568976}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagao Kaidō|qq=|1=Nagao Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651896}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōtate Shrine|qq=|1=Hochigai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504380}}, and passed west of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Hanzei|Hanzei]]'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-ga-ue Park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|qq=|1=Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520136}}, now part of [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]].
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|qq=}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from [[:en:Sakaihigashi Station|Sakaihigashi Station]] on the [[:en:Koya Line|Nankai Kōya Line]], operated by [[:en:Nankai Electric Railway|Nankai Electric Railway]]. ** From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|qq=|1=Sugawara Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48532247}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanataguchi Station|qq=|1=Hanataguchi Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615569}} on the [[:en:Hankai Line|Hankai Line]], operated by [[:en:Hankai Tramway|Hankai Tramway]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:境王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11429062]] Wikidata name: Sakai-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}]] | location = [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|WD=Q1042008}} }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}''' (境王子) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, part of the historical [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitadaidei-chō|1=Kitadaidei-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131912466}} in [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]]. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], and [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]].
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ([[:en:Eihō|Eihō]] 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)]] in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdōri-suji|1=Daido-suji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439390}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). In 1908 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社 (堺市)|lt=Sugawara Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|1=Sugawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619211}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōdō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568976}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagao Kaidō|1=Nagao Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651896}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōtate Shrine|1=Hochigai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504380}}, and passed west of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Hanzei|Hanzei]]'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|1=Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520136}}, now part of [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]].
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|qq=}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from [[:en:Sakaihigashi Station|Sakaihigashi Station]] on the [[:en:Koya Line|Nankai Kōya Line]], operated by [[:en:Nankai Electric Railway|Nankai Electric Railway]]. ** From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|1=Sugawara Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48532247}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanataguchi Station|1=Hanataguchi Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615569}} on the [[:en:Hankai Line|Hankai Line]], operated by [[:en:Hankai Tramway|Hankai Tramway]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:境王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11429062]] Wikidata name: Sakai-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}]] | location = [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|WD=Q1042008}} }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}''' (境王子) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, part of the historical [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitadaidei-chō|1=Kitadaidei-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131912466}} in [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]]. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], and [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]].
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ([[:en:Eihō|Eihō]] 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)]] in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdōri-suji|1=Daido-suji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439390}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). In 1908 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社 (堺市)|lt=Sugawara Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|1=Sugawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619211}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōdō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568976}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagao Kaidō|1=Nagao Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651896}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōtate Shrine|1=Hochigai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504380}}, and passed west of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Hanzei|Hanzei]]'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|1=Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520136}}, now part of [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]].
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|qq=}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from [[:en:Sakaihigashi Station|Sakaihigashi Station]] on the [[:en:Koya Line|Nankai Kōya Line]], operated by [[:en:Nankai Electric Railway|Nankai Electric Railway]]. ** From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|1=Sugawara Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48532247}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanataguchi Station|1=Hanataguchi Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615569}} on the [[:en:Hankai Line|Hankai Line]], operated by [[:en:Hankai Tramway|Hankai Tramway]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:境王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11429062]] Wikidata name: Sakai-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}]] | location = [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|WD=Q1042008}} }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}''' (境王子) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, part of the historical [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitadaidei-chō|1=Kitadaidei-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131912466}} in [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]]. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], and [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]].
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ([[:en:Eihō|Eihō]] 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)]] in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdōri-suji|1=Daido-suji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439390}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). In 1908 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社 (堺市)|lt=Sugawara Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|1=Sugawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619211}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōdō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568976}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagao Kaidō|1=Nagao Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651896}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōtate Shrine|1=Hochigai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504380}}, and passed west of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Hanzei|Hanzei]]'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|1=Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520136}}, now part of [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]].
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|qq=}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from [[:en:Sakaihigashi Station|Sakaihigashi Station]] on the [[:en:Koya Line|Nankai Kōya Line]], operated by [[:en:Nankai Electric Railway|Nankai Electric Railway]]. ** From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|1=Sugawara Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48532247}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanataguchi Station|1=Hanataguchi Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615569}} on the [[:en:Hankai Line|Hankai Line]], operated by [[:en:Hankai Tramway|Hankai Tramway]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:境王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11429062]] Wikidata name: Sakai-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}]] | location = [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|WD=Q1042008}} }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}''' (境王子) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, part of the historical [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitadaidei-chō|1=Kitadaidei-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131912466}} in [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]]. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], and [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]].
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ([[:en:Eihō|Eihō]] 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)]] in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdōri-suji|1=Daido-suji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439390}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). In 1908 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社 (堺市)|lt=Sugawara Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|1=Sugawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619211}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōdō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568976}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagao Kaidō|1=Nagao Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651896}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōtate Shrine|1=Hochigai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504380}}, and passed west of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Hanzei|Hanzei]]'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|1=Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520136}}, now part of [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]].
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|qq=}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from [[:en:Sakaihigashi Station|Sakaihigashi Station]] on the [[:en:Koya Line|Nankai Kōya Line]], operated by [[:en:Nankai Electric Railway|Nankai Electric Railway]]. ** From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|1=Sugawara Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48532247}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanataguchi Station|1=Hanataguchi Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615569}} on the [[:en:Hankai Line|Hankai Line]], operated by [[:en:Hankai Tramway|Hankai Tramway]].
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori Ōji|1=Tsumori Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555995}} * '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:境王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11429062]] Wikidata name: Sakai-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}]] | location = [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|WD=Q1042008}} }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}''' (境王子) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, part of the historical [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitadaidei-chō|1=Kitadaidei-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131912466}} in [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]]. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], and [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]].
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ([[:en:Eihō|Eihō]] 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)]] in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdōri-suji|1=Daido-suji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439390}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). In 1908 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社 (堺市)|lt=Sugawara Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|1=Sugawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619211}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōdō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568976}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagao Kaidō|1=Nagao Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651896}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōtate Shrine|1=Hochigai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504380}}, and passed west of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Hanzei|Hanzei]]'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|1=Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520136}}, now part of [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]].
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|qq=}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from [[:en:Sakaihigashi Station|Sakaihigashi Station]] on the [[:en:Koya Line|Nankai Kōya Line]], operated by [[:en:Nankai Electric Railway|Nankai Electric Railway]]. ** From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|1=Sugawara Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48532247}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanataguchi Station|1=Hanataguchi Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615569}} on the [[:en:Hankai Line|Hankai Line]], operated by [[:en:Hankai Tramway|Hankai Tramway]].
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori Ōji|1=Tsumori Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555995}} * '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:境王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11429062]] Wikidata name: Sakai-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}]] | location = [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|WD=Q1042008}} }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}''' (境王子) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, part of the historical [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitadaidei-chō|1=Kitadaidei-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131912466}} in [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]]. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], and [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]].
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ([[:en:Eihō|Eihō]] 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)]] in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdōri-suji|1=Daido-suji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439390}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). In 1908 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社 (堺市)|lt=Sugawara Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|1=Sugawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619211}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōdō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568976}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagao Kaidō|1=Nagao Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651896}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōtate Shrine|1=Hochigai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504380}}, and passed west of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Hanzei|Hanzei]]'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|1=Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520136}}, now part of [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]].
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|qq=}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from [[:en:Sakaihigashi Station|Sakaihigashi Station]] on the [[:en:Koya Line|Nankai Kōya Line]], operated by [[:en:Nankai Electric Railway|Nankai Electric Railway]]. ** From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|1=Sugawara Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48532247}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanataguchi Station|1=Hanataguchi Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615569}} on the [[:en:Hankai Line|Hankai Line]], operated by [[:en:Hankai Tramway|Hankai Tramway]].
