<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:境王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11429062]] Wikidata name: Sakai-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}]] | location = [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|WD=Q1042008}} }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}''' (境王子) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, part of the historical [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitadaidei-chō|1=Kitadaidei-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131912466}} in [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]]. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], and [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]].
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ([[:en:Eihō|Eihō]] 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)]] in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdōri-suji|1=Daido-suji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439390}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). In 1908 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社 (堺市)|lt=Sugawara Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|1=Sugawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619211}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōdō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568976}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagao Kaidō|1=Nagao Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651896}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōtate Shrine|1=Hochigai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504380}}, and passed west of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Hanzei|Hanzei]]'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|1=Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520136}}, now part of [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]].
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|qq=}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from [[:en:Sakaihigashi Station|Sakaihigashi Station]] on the [[:en:Koya Line|Nankai Kōya Line]], operated by [[:en:Nankai Electric Railway|Nankai Electric Railway]]. ** From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|1=Sugawara Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48532247}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanataguchi Station|1=Hanataguchi Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615569}} on the [[:en:Hankai Line|Hankai Line]], operated by [[:en:Hankai Tramway|Hankai Tramway]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:境王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11429062]] Wikidata name: Sakai-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}]] | location = [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|WD=Q1042008}} }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}''' (境王子) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, part of the historical [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitadaidei-chō|1=Kitadaidei-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131912466}} in [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]]. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], and [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]].
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ([[:en:Eihō|Eihō]] 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)]] in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdōri-suji|1=Daido-suji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439390}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). In 1908 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社 (堺市)|lt=Sugawara Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|1=Sugawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619211}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōdō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568976}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagao Kaidō|1=Nagao Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651896}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōtate Shrine|1=Hochigai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504380}}, and passed west of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Hanzei|Hanzei]]'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|1=Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520136}}, now part of [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]].
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|qq=}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from [[:en:Sakaihigashi Station|Sakaihigashi Station]] on the [[:en:Koya Line|Nankai Kōya Line]], operated by [[:en:Nankai Electric Railway|Nankai Electric Railway]]. ** From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|1=Sugawara Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48532247}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanataguchi Station|1=Hanataguchi Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615569}} on the [[:en:Hankai Line|Hankai Line]], operated by [[:en:Hankai Tramway|Hankai Tramway]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:境王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11429062]] Wikidata name: Sakai-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}]] | location = [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|WD=Q1042008}} }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}''' (境王子) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, part of the historical [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitadaidei-chō|1=Kitadaidei-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131912466}} in [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]]. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], and [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]].
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ([[:en:Eihō|Eihō]] 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)]] in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdōri-suji|1=Daido-suji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439390}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). In 1908 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社 (堺市)|lt=Sugawara Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|1=Sugawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619211}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōdō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568976}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagao Kaidō|1=Nagao Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651896}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōtate Shrine|1=Hochigai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504380}}, and passed west of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Hanzei|Hanzei]]'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|1=Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520136}}, now part of [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]].
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|qq=}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from [[:en:Sakaihigashi Station|Sakaihigashi Station]] on the [[:en:Koya Line|Nankai Kōya Line]], operated by [[:en:Nankai Electric Railway|Nankai Electric Railway]]. ** From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|1=Sugawara Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48532247}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanataguchi Station|1=Hanataguchi Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615569}} on the [[:en:Hankai Line|Hankai Line]], operated by [[:en:Hankai Tramway|Hankai Tramway]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:境王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11429062]] Wikidata name: Sakai-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}]] | location = [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|WD=Q1042008}} }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}''' (境王子) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, part of the historical [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitadaidei-chō|1=Kitadaidei-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131912466}} in [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]]. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], and [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]].
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ([[:en:Eihō|Eihō]] 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)]] in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdōri-suji|1=Daido-suji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439390}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). In 1908 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社 (堺市)|lt=Sugawara Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|1=Sugawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619211}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōdō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568976}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagao Kaidō|1=Nagao Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651896}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōtate Shrine|1=Hochigai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504380}}, and passed west of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Hanzei|Hanzei]]'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|1=Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520136}}, now part of [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]].
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|qq=}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from [[:en:Sakaihigashi Station|Sakaihigashi Station]] on the [[:en:Koya Line|Nankai Kōya Line]], operated by [[:en:Nankai Electric Railway|Nankai Electric Railway]]. ** From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|1=Sugawara Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48532247}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanataguchi Station|1=Hanataguchi Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615569}} on the [[:en:Hankai Line|Hankai Line]], operated by [[:en:Hankai Tramway|Hankai Tramway]].
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori Ōji|1=Tsumori Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555995}} * '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:境王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:境王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11429062]] Wikidata name: Sakai-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}]] | location = [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|WD=Q1042008}} }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}''' (境王子) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, part of the historical [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitadaidei-chō|1=Kitadaidei-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131912466}} in [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]]. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], and [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]].
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ([[:en:Eihō|Eihō]] 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)]] in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdōri-suji|1=Daido-suji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439390}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). In 1908 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社 (堺市)|lt=Sugawara Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|1=Sugawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619211}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōdō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568976}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagao Kaidō|1=Nagao Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651896}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōtate Shrine|1=Hochigai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504380}}, and passed west of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Hanzei|Hanzei]]'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|1=Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520136}}, now part of [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]].
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|qq=}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from [[:en:Sakaihigashi Station|Sakaihigashi Station]] on the [[:en:Koya Line|Nankai Kōya Line]], operated by [[:en:Nankai Electric Railway|Nankai Electric Railway]]. ** From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|1=Sugawara Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48532247}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanataguchi Station|1=Hanataguchi Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615569}} on the [[:en:Hankai Line|Hankai Line]], operated by [[:en:Hankai Tramway|Hankai Tramway]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:境王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:境王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:境王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11429062]] Wikidata name: Sakai-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}]] | location = [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|WD=Q1042008}} }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}''' (境王子) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, part of the historical [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitadaidei-chō|1=Kitadaidei-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131912466}} in [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]]. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], and [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]].
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ([[:en:Eihō|Eihō]] 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)]] in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdōri-suji|1=Daido-suji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439390}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). In 1908 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社 (堺市)|lt=Sugawara Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|1=Sugawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619211}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōdō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568976}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagao Kaidō|1=Nagao Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651896}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōtate Shrine|1=Hochigai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504380}}, and passed west of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Hanzei|Hanzei]]'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|1=Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520136}}, now part of [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]].
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|qq=}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from [[:en:Sakaihigashi Station|Sakaihigashi Station]] on the [[:en:Koya Line|Nankai Kōya Line]], operated by [[:en:Nankai Electric Railway|Nankai Electric Railway]]. ** From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|1=Sugawara Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48532247}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanataguchi Station|1=Hanataguchi Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615569}} on the [[:en:Hankai Line|Hankai Line]], operated by [[:en:Hankai Tramway|Hankai Tramway]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:境王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:境王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:境王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11429062]] Wikidata name: Sakai-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}]] | location = [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|WD=Q1042008}} }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}''' (境王子) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, part of the historical [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitadaidei-chō|1=Kitadaidei-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131912466}} in [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]]. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], and [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]].
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ([[:en:Eihō|Eihō]] 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)]] in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdōri-suji|1=Daido-suji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439390}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). In 1908 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社 (堺市)|lt=Sugawara Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|1=Sugawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619211}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōdō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568976}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagao Kaidō|1=Nagao Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651896}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōtate Shrine|1=Hochigai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504380}}, and passed west of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Hanzei|Hanzei]]'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|1=Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520136}}, now part of [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]].
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|qq=}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from [[:en:Sakaihigashi Station|Sakaihigashi Station]] on the [[:en:Koya Line|Nankai Kōya Line]], operated by [[:en:Nankai Electric Railway|Nankai Electric Railway]]. ** From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|1=Sugawara Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48532247}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanataguchi Station|1=Hanataguchi Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615569}} on the [[:en:Hankai Line|Hankai Line]], operated by [[:en:Hankai Tramway|Hankai Tramway]].
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori Ōji|1=Tsumori Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555995}} * '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:境王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:境王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:境王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11429062]] Wikidata name: Sakai-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}]] | location = [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|WD=Q1042008}} }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}''' (境王子) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, part of the historical [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitadaidei-chō|1=Kitadaidei-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131912466}} in [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]]. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], and [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]].
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ([[:en:Eihō|Eihō]] 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)]] in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdōri-suji|1=Daido-suji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439390}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). In 1908 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社 (堺市)|lt=Sugawara Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|1=Sugawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619211}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōdō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568976}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagao Kaidō|1=Nagao Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651896}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōtate Shrine|1=Hochigai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504380}}, and passed west of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Hanzei|Hanzei]]'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|1=Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520136}}, now part of [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]].
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|qq=}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from [[:en:Sakaihigashi Station|Sakaihigashi Station]] on the [[:en:Koya Line|Nankai Kōya Line]], operated by [[:en:Nankai Electric Railway|Nankai Electric Railway]]. ** From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|1=Sugawara Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48532247}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanataguchi Station|1=Hanataguchi Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615569}} on the [[:en:Hankai Line|Hankai Line]], operated by [[:en:Hankai Tramway|Hankai Tramway]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:境王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:境王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:境王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11429062]] Wikidata name: Sakai-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}]] | location = [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|WD=Q1042008}} }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}''' (境王子) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, part of the historical [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitadaidei-chō|1=Kitadaidei-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131912466}} in [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]]. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], and [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]].
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ([[:en:Eihō|Eihō]] 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)]] in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdōri-suji|1=Daido-suji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439390}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). In 1908 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社 (堺市)|lt=Sugawara Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|1=Sugawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619211}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōdō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568976}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagao Kaidō|1=Nagao Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651896}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōtate Shrine|1=Hochigai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504380}}, and passed west of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Hanzei|Hanzei]]'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|1=Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520136}}, now part of [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]].
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|qq=}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from [[:en:Sakaihigashi Station|Sakaihigashi Station]] on the [[:en:Koya Line|Nankai Kōya Line]], operated by [[:en:Nankai Electric Railway|Nankai Electric Railway]]. ** From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|1=Sugawara Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48532247}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanataguchi Station|1=Hanataguchi Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615569}} on the [[:en:Hankai Line|Hankai Line]], operated by [[:en:Hankai Tramway|Hankai Tramway]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:境王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:境王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:境王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11429062]] Wikidata name: Sakai-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}]] | location = [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|WD=Q1042008}} }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}''' (境王子) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, part of the historical [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitadaidei-chō|1=Kitadaidei-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131912466}} in [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]]. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], and [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]].
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ([[:en:Eihō|Eihō]] 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Sakai)]] in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdōri-suji|1=Daido-suji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439390}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). In 1908 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社 (堺市)|lt=Sugawara Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|1=Sugawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619211}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōdō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568976}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagao Kaidō|1=Nagao Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651896}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōtate Shrine|1=Hochigai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504380}}, and passed west of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Hanzei|Hanzei]]'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|1=Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520136}}, now part of [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe, Wakayama]].
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|qq=}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from [[:en:Sakaihigashi Station|Sakaihigashi Station]] on the [[:en:Koya Line|Nankai Kōya Line]], operated by [[:en:Nankai Electric Railway|Nankai Electric Railway]]. ** From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|1=Sugawara Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48532247}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanataguchi Station|1=Hanataguchi Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615569}} on the [[:en:Hankai Line|Hankai Line]], operated by [[:en:Hankai Tramway|Hankai Tramway]].

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:境王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:境王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:境王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11429062]] Wikidata name: Sakai-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}]] | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|en|Sakai-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1042008|lt=Sakai-ku}}, {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|WD=Q1042008}} }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}''' (境王子) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitadaidei-chō|1=Kitadaidei-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131912466}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|en|Sakai-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1042008|lt=Sakai-ku}}, {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai, Osaka}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|WD=Q1047117|lt=Izumi Province}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|en|Eihō|WD=Q1193881|lt=Eihō}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano shrine|en|Kumano shrine|WD=Q3200355|lt=Kumano Shrine (Sakai)}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdōri-suji|1=Daido-suji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439390}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社 (堺市)|lt=Sugawara Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|1=Sugawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619211}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōdō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568976}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagao Kaidō|1=Nagao Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651896}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōtate Shrine|1=Hochigai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504380}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Hanzei|en|Emperor Hanzei|WD=Q329769|lt=Hanzei}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|en|Kumano Hongū Taisha|WD=Q705035|lt=Kumano Hongū Taisha}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|1=Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520136}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|en|Tanabe, Wakayama|WD=Q304266|lt=Tanabe, Wakayama}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|qq=}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|en|Sakaihigashi Station|WD=Q7402783|lt=Sakaihigashi Station}} on the {{ill|Koya Line|en|Koya Line|WD=Q71986|lt=Nankai Kōya Line}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|en|Nankai Electric Railway|WD=Q1188230|lt=Nankai Electric Railway}}. ** From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|1=Sugawara Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48532247}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanataguchi Station|1=Hanataguchi Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615569}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|en|Hankai Line|WD=Q5648522|lt=Hankai Line}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|en|Hankai Tramway|WD=Q2069534|lt=Hankai Tramway}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori Ōji|1=Tsumori Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555995}} * '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:境王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:境王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:境王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11429062]] Wikidata name: Sakai-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}} | image = [[File:境王子跡.jpg|250px|thumb|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}]] | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|en|Sakai-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1042008|lt=Sakai-ku}}, {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|WD=Q1042008}} }}
'''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}''' (境王子) was the 7th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines, part of the historical {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage routes.
== location == The shrine is believed to have been located near the current {{ill|Kitadaidei-chō|ja|北田出井町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitadaidei-chō|1=Kitadaidei-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131912466}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|en|Sakai-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1042008|lt=Sakai-ku}}, {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai, Osaka}}. The name "Sakai" (meaning "border") is derived from the area's historical position at the boundary of the ancient provinces of {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province}}, and {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|WD=Q1047117|lt=Izumi Province}}.
References to Sakai Ōji appear in historical documents, such as the 1081 ({{ill|Eihō|en|Eihō|WD=Q1193881|lt=Eihō}} 1) record 'Fujiwara no Tamefusa Kyōki' (藤原為房卿記), where it is referred to as "Wakasaikai no Shōdō" (和泉堺之小堂), marking the first appearance of the name "Sakai" in historical texts.
The shrine was later relocated to the {{ill|Kumano shrine|en|Kumano shrine|WD=Q3200355|lt=Kumano Shrine (Sakai)}} in Yuyachō, Sakai (now along {{ill|Ōdōri-suji|ja|大道筋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdōri-suji|1=Daido-suji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439390}} in {{ill|Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja|熊野町東1丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanochō Higashi 1-chōme|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). In 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 41), this Kumano Shrine was merged with {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社 (堺市)|lt=Sugawara Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|1=Sugawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619211}} (located at 2-1-38 Ebisunochō Higashi, Sakai-ku), leaving the original site of Sakai Ōji uncertain.
However, historical documents indicate that the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野街道|1=Kumano Kaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kōdō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568976}} passed through the eastern part of Sakai, intersecting with the {{ill|Nagao Kaidō|ja|長尾街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagao Kaidō|1=Nagao Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651896}} near {{ill|Hōtate Shrine|ja|方違神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōtate Shrine|1=Hochigai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504380}}, and passed west of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Hanzei|en|Emperor Hanzei|WD=Q329769|lt=Hanzei}}'s tomb. Based on these clues, Sakai Ōji is believed to have been located just north of Hōtate Shrine. The {{ill|Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja|文久三年改正堺大絵図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū 3 Revised Sakai Great Map|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} even includes references to "Oguri-michi" (小栗道) and "Ōji-ga-ue" (王子ヶ飢), hinting at the shrine’s historical presence.
Today, a stone monument marking the "Site of Sakai Ōji" stands in {{ill|Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja|王子ヶ飢公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-ga-ue Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (3-4-6 Kitadaidei-chō, Sakai-ku), donated from {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|en|Kumano Hongū Taisha|WD=Q705035|lt=Kumano Hongū Taisha}} in {{ill|Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|ja|本宮町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|1=Hongū, Tanabe, Wakayama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520136}}, now part of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|en|Tanabe, Wakayama|WD=Q304266|lt=Tanabe, Wakayama}}.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:境王子跡.jpg|Site of {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|qq=}} </gallery>
== Access == * '''Ōji-ga-ue Park:''' About 850m northeast from {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|en|Sakaihigashi Station|WD=Q7402783|lt=Sakaihigashi Station}} on the {{ill|Koya Line|en|Koya Line|WD=Q71986|lt=Nankai Kōya Line}}, operated by {{ill|Nankai Electric Railway|en|Nankai Electric Railway|WD=Q1188230|lt=Nankai Electric Railway}}. ** From Sakaihigashi Station, head north along the railway towards Namba, and turn east after the third railway crossing. * '''{{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|ja|菅原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Sakai)|1=Sugawara Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48532247}}:''' About 300m southeast from {{ill|Hanataguchi Station|ja|花田口停留場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanataguchi Station|1=Hanataguchi Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615569}} on the {{ill|Hankai Line|en|Hankai Line|WD=Q5648522|lt=Hankai Line}}, operated by {{ill|Hankai Tramway|en|Hankai Tramway|WD=Q2069534|lt=Hankai Tramway}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} *: {{ill|Tsumori Ōji|ja|津守王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori Ōji|1=Tsumori Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555995}} * '''{{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}}''' - {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}}
